description: An invariant classified as 'flappy' by compute-invariant-efficacy.py (fires repeatedly without being cited in any fix commit over RECENT_COMMITS scan) is a candidate for retirement or recalibration. Pattern first applied R22 to file-written-has-source-majority. Arc IV's equivalent of Arc II's retire-legacy-override pattern, applied to the invariant battery itself.

trigger.check: python3 -c "import json, os; p='metrics/hme-invariant-efficacy.json'; d=json.load(open(p)) if os.path.isfile(p) else {}; c=d.get('retirement_candidates',[]); print(','.join(c)) if c else None"
trigger.matches_when: retirement_candidates non-empty; stdout names flappy invariants ready for retirement/recalibration

steps: For each candidate: read its full definition in tools/HME/config/invariants.json.
Decide between (a) retire outright, (b) recalibrate threshold, (c) convert to trend-based (see predictions-log-gap-bounded R19 example).
If retire: delete the invariant block, append to metrics/hme-invariant-retirement-log.jsonl with {ts, id, rounds_flappy, reason, retired_in, commit}.
If recalibrate: edit the shell/threshold, keep the id, document the change in the description.
Verify next efficacy report shows candidate is no longer flappy.

history.fallback: file-written-module-sane with require_fields:['source'] covers underlying signal
